lines: The planets of our solar system. Smallest to largest.
Mercury. The smallest planet. Just 4,879 kilometers across.
Venus. Almost the same size as Earth. 12,104 kilometers.
Neptune. The first ice giant. 49,528 kilometers. Nearly 4 times wider than Earth.
Saturn. Famous for its rings. 116,460 kilometers across.
Jupiter. The king of planets. 139,820 kilometers. You could fit every other planet inside it.

seo.title: Solar System Size Comparison — Smallest to Largest 🪐
